full_prompt: Design one representative single-room community tornado safe room, 12.2 m x 12.2 m x 3.0 m clear interior (about 149 m2, occupancy ~320 persons at the FEMA P-361 standing-occupancy 0.46 m2/person / 5 sq ft/person rate), as a site-cast reinforced-concrete shear-wall box (RC walls, RC roof slab, RC floor slab) fully compliant with FEMA P-361 Safe Rooms for Tornadoes and Hurricanes (4th ed., 2021) and ICC 500. The shelter must resist a 250 mph (112 m/s) 3-second gust tornado design wind applied as ASCE 7 / ICC 500 equivalent static pressures on Exposure C; a tornado debris missile consisting of a 15-lb (6.8 kg) nominal Douglas-fir 2x4 traveling at 100 mph (44.7 m/s) horizontally against any wall and 67 mph (30.0 m/s) vertically against the roof, impacting at any location; and an alternate-load-path progressive-collapse check in which any single primary column or an equivalent 6 m length of primary wall is notionally removed and the remaining structure must bridge the removal without collapse under 1.2 Dead + 0.5 Live per GSA / DoD UFC 4-023-03 alternate-path procedure. Walls are 8.0 in (203 mm) thick normal-weight reinforced concrete with fc >= 4000 psi (27.6 MPa) and Grade 60 rebar (fy = 420 MPa); roof is 8.0 in (203 mm) RC two-way slab. The structure is anchored to a continuous RC mat foundation designed to resist all factored overturning and uplift from the design wind. Minimum three egress / entry openings are required per P-361 occupancy rules; each door opening is reinforced by RC jambs and a header, and the door assemblies themselves are separately tested (out of scope of this geometry design). Deliverable is a single assembled CAD model of the RC shell including walls, roof, floor, mat foundation, rebar cage, door openings with reinforcement, suitable for (a) linear + nonlinear static structural FEA under design wind, (b) explicit-dynamics debris-impact analysis, (c) alternate-load-path progressive-collapse analysis. Vibration, thermal, fluid, radiation, and buckling analyses are NOT required.
short_prompt:
Design a single-room community tornado safe room sized for ~320 standing occupants (FEMA P-361 0.46 m^2/person), built as a site-cast RC shear-wall box on an RC mat foundation with at least three egress openings, per FEMA P-361 (4th ed., 2021) and ICC 500. 8 in RC walls + 8 in two-way RC roof slab, 4000 psi concrete, Grade 60 rebar; tested ICC 500 door assemblies out of scope.

Envelope: 12.2 x 12.2 x 3.0 m clear interior (~149 m^2) with reinforced jambs at openings.

Load cases.
- LC1 design wind: 250 mph 3-sec gust on Exposure C as ASCE 7 / ICC 500 equivalent static pressures on walls and roof.
- LC2 debris missile horizontal: 15 lb 2x4 at 100 mph on any wall.
- LC3 debris missile vertical: 15 lb 2x4 at 67 mph on the roof.
- LC4 progressive-collapse alternate path: notionally remove one primary column or a 6 m length of primary wall and check stability under 1.2D + 0.5L per GSA 2016 / UFC 4-023-03.

Debris-missile impact uses an explicit-dynamics solver; alternate-load-path uses a nonlinear static procedure.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: 15-lb 2x4 missile impact at 100/67 mph requires transient dynamics with material damage; cannot be done with linear static FEA.
P-361 progressive-collapse / robustness check requires notional element removal + nonlinear static or nonlinear dynamic analysis (GSA / UFC 4-023-03).

prompt.title: FEMA P-361 community tornado safe room (~320-occupant)
prompt.brief: Design a 12.2 x 12.2 x 3.0 m RC community safe room to P-361 with 250 mph gust + 15-lb 2x4 debris at 100/67 mph + alternate-load-path progressive collapse.

load_cases.name: design_wind
load_cases.application: ICC 500 equivalent static wall + roof pressures

load_cases.name: progressive_collapse_alternate_path
load_cases.magnitude: remove one primary column or 6 m primary wall segment; loading 1.2D + 0.5L
load_cases.application: notional element removal per GSA / UFC 4-023-03

boundary_conditions.foundation_interface: mat foundation fully resisting wind uplift + overturning

pass_fail_criteria.limit: structure remains stable after notional element removal under 1.2D + 0.5L

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: FEMA P-361 standing-occupancy density (5 sq ft / person)

exclusions_explained.buckling_analysis: Short thick RC walls not buckling-critical.

eval_coverage: FEMA P-361 exact numeric design envelope
